body.title: blame_with_model.py — git blame enriched with Co-Authored-By model attribution
body.result: blame_with_model.py — git blame + Co-Authored-By → model attribution (opus/sonnet/haiku detection, fallback to coauthor name). --model-only filter, --json output, message cache. 16 tests, 4165 regression pass. Live spot-check on consult.py confirms working.
body.description:
Multi-model debugging requires knowing which model authored a given chunk of code. Co-Authored-By trailers already capture this in commits (e.g. 'Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context)'). Build lab/claudecode/blame_with_model.py that runs git blame on a file (or a line range), looks up each commit's Co-Authored-By trailer, and outputs blame inline with model attribution.

Output format:
  <line>  <commit-short>  <author>  <model>  <code>

Optional --model-only flag to filter to lines from a specific model (e.g. --model-only sonnet to find Sonnet-authored hot spots).

**Affected files:** new lab/claudecode/blame_with_model.py + tests/test_blame_with_model.py.
**Design rules:** none apply (read-only diagnostic).
**Scope boundary:** in scope: blame enrichment script + tests. Out of scope: editing source files to add per-line attribution comments (separate ticket — T-author-model-header).
**Test plan:** unit tests with mocked git commands; one integration test against the actual repo's git log.
